sn48.42:1.2: Then Uṇṇābha the brahman went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
sn48.42:1.3: As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
sn48.42:2.1: “Master Gotama, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and do not experience one another’s range or domain.
sn48.42:2.3: The faculty of the eye, the faculty of the ear, the faculty of the nose, the faculty of the tongue, & the faculty of the body.
sn48.42:2.4: Now, of these five faculties that have different domains, different ranges, and do not experience one another’s range or domain, what is their arbitrator? And what experiences their range & domain?”
sn48.42:3.1: “Brahman, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and do not experience one another’s range or domain.
sn48.42:3.3: The faculty of the eye, the faculty of the ear, the faculty of the nose, the faculty of the tongue, & the faculty of the body.
sn48.42:3.4: Now, of these five faculties that have different domains, different ranges, and do not experience one another’s range or domain, the intellect is their arbitrator. The intellect experiences their range & domain.”
sn48.42:4.1: “And what, Master Gotama, is the arbitrator of the intellect?”
sn48.42:4.2: “Mindfulness, brahman, is the arbitrator of the intellect.”
sn48.42:4.3: “And what, Master Gotama, is the arbitrator of mindfulness?”
sn48.42:4.4: “Release, brahman, is the arbitrator of mindfulness.”
sn48.42:4.5: “And what, Master Gotama, is the arbitrator of release?”
sn48.42:4.6: “Unbinding, brahman, is the arbitrator of release.”
sn48.42:4.7: “And what, Master Gotama, is the arbitrator of unbinding?”
sn48.42:4.8: “You’ve gone too far, brahman. You can’t keep holding on up to the limit of questions.
sn48.42:4.9: For the holy life is lived to gain a footing in unbinding, to culminate in unbinding, to have unbinding as its final end.”
sn48.42:5.1: Then Uṇṇābha the brahman, delighting & rejoicing in the Blessed One’s words, rose from his seat, and—after bowing down to him & circumambulating him—left.
sn48.42:6.1: Then, not long after he had left, the Blessed One addressed the monks,
sn48.42:6.2: “Suppose, monks, that there were a gabled house or gabled hall with a window on the east. When the sun rises and a sunbeam enters the window, where does it land?” [literally, “where is it established?”]
sn48.42:6.4: “In the same way, monks, Uṇṇābha the brahman’s conviction in the Tathāgata is settled, rooted, established, firm. It cannot be shaken by a contemplative or a brahman or a deva or a Māra or a Brahmā or anyone at all in the cosmos.
sn48.42:6.5: If he were to die at this time, there is no fetter, fettered by which, Uṇṇābha the brahman would come back to this world.”
